Kumo

Kumo 2.5.2 · evidence catalog

Canonical React in, normalized framework surfaces out

Kumo starts from the Kumo React components as its canonical inputs. Normalization captures their structure, states, semantics, and behavior in a framework-neutral form; the current catalog then exercises React, Vue, Svelte, and Solid output surfaces.

Receipt-derived status and limitations

react41 blocked
vue41 verified
svelte41 verified
solid41 verified

41/41 Vue outputs are framework-native. The other 0/41 are legacy outputs. React is canonical input, but its audit currently credits only checks bound to an exact source/package revision; build pages and lookalikes do not count as canonical evidence.

What “proved” means here

The exact catalog gates are: all four runtime routes build; server-rendered markup and expected semantic/ARIA state are present; framework hydration source and hydrated DOM are present where the family proof requires them; required interaction/state tokens pass family-specific checks; screenshots are non-empty and their SHA-256 pixels match across React, Vue, Svelte, and Solid; selected proofs also require repeated dev/production render hashes to match. Bundle bytes are read from each built JavaScript asset. Build milliseconds and bundle bytes below are measured values recorded in benchmarks/catalog.json, not estimates.

react416.4 ms mean build7,666.5 kB catalog JS total
vue441.8 ms mean build2,559.2 kB catalog JS total
svelte502 ms mean build1,263.2 kB catalog JS total
solid522.6 ms mean build442.7 kB catalog JS total

How to read the evidence

The root is the single catalog. Every row links to a component subpage and reports its four framework measurements inline. Measurements describe these checked-in proof fixtures on the recorded catalog source commit 37e7f75; they are not application-wide performance forecasts. Pixel equality does not prove accessibility, behavioral completeness, source quality, or equivalent runtime internals.
